original_hypothesis.title: VestingDeployer deployVesting event parameter misuse may break off-chain indexing and allow wrong vestingId association
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: SecondSwap_VestingDeployer.deployVesting emits VestingDeployed(token, vesting, vestingId) where vestingId is user-supplied string. If off-chain systems treat vestingId as authoritative identifier without ensuring uniqueness or binding it to (token,start,end,steps), a token issuer can emit misleading IDs (duplicates, collisions, impersonation), causing indexers/UI to display incorrect plan details and potentially directing users to interact with unintended vesting contracts.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Although not an on-chain fund drain by itself, this is a realistic production attack against protocol integrity relying on event indexing, especially for marketplaces and vesting registries. Needs validation of how the protocol consumes vestingId.
deployment_assumptions: Frontends/indexers use vestingId from events as a unique or trusted identifier
No on-chain registry enforces vestingId uniqueness
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No contract-level storage tracks vestingId uniqueness; vestingId is an arbitrary string in the event only.

proof_of_signal.pattern: event VestingDeployed(..., string vestingId) emitted from user-supplied vestingId without uniqueness enforcement

preconditions: Attacker is authorized token owner in _tokenOwner mapping
Off-chain infra uses vestingId as trusted identifier

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: untrusted identifier only emitted in event
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: indexing/UI confusion, impersonation of legitimate vesting plans
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no vestingId registry or uniqueness constraint

analysis_questions: How do protocol indexers map vesting contracts—by address only or by vestingId?
Is vestingId expected to be unique and human-meaningful?
Are there UI warnings/bindings to token address and schedule params?
validation_requirements: Review subgraph/indexer code for keying logic
Attempt deploying two vestings with same vestingId and observe UI behavior
Confirm whether any on-chain verification uses vestingId (likely none)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Issuer deploys a malicious vesting plan but uses a vestingId matching a legitimate plan name; UI lists it as the legitimate plan and users may trade/list against the wrong contract address.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Medium—users may interact with wrong vesting contract, leading to wrong economic decisions/trades
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: UI users relying on vestingId labeling
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Medium—can fix indexer to key by address and display immutable schedule params

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests around event-driven indexing assumptions

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: monitor duplicate vestingId emissions for same token

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
